trace_table.claim: SEO metadata describes the About page as Women in Aviation Middle East, a Middle East chapter run by volunteers opening doors for women across aviation and aerospace.

trace_table.claim: A regional aviation community that opens doors.

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME is the Middle East chapter of Women in Aviation International and supports women across aviation and aerospace roles in the region.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About: Who we are

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME's hub is Dubai, members live and work across more than forty countries, and volunteers run the organisation.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About: Who we are

trace_table.claim: At-a-glance stats: since 2013, 1,300+ members, 40+ countries, and 750+ attendees at the 2019 conference.

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME started in May 2013 at the Dubai Airport Show, first board meeting October 2013, WAI International recognition November 2014, and association with The Emirates Aerosports Federation in Dubai.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About: Our story; 01 Organization/01 Legal Standing & Chapter Authority (Decision).md

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME has appeared at Dubai Airshow, Abu Dhabi Air Expo, MEBAA, Airport Show in Dubai, Global Aerospace Summit, and Kuwait Aviation Show, and by 2019 its conference drew 750+ attendees, 26 international speakers, and a dozen sponsors.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, About: Our story and Impact

staging_allowances.claim: Six board portraits are real board members but dated current-site photos pending refreshed consented portraits; page marker says board photos are from the current site and will be refreshed before launch.
Ambassador cards use sample names, roles, and one-line bios; the section marker says sample cards below and each card carries a Sample tag.

summary: All material claims on the built /about page trace to the vault, and the brand locks hold. The page has two valid staging allowances for dated board photos and sample Ambassador cards.
